digital_workers.name: Junior Engineer
digital_workers.description: Handles routine engineering review tasks including design brief analysis, drawing checks, and document compliance.

jobs.name: Review Design Brief
jobs.description: End-to-end review of a project design brief to confirm all required documents are present, correctly classified, and meet minimum content standards.

tasks.name: Review and Identify Design Brief Documents
tasks.description: Upload the design brief package and identify all submitted documents. Extract key metadata, classify each document by type, and flag any missing or incomplete items against standard brief requirements.

steps.name: Extract and analyse design brief documents
steps.description: Upload the design brief package (PDF, DOCX, or ZIP of documents) to the Content Reviewer Agent. Extract key metadata from each document including document title, document type (e.g. Architectural Brief, Structural Brief, Services Brief, Town Planning Report), author, date, project name, and project number. Produce a structured list of all identified documents.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with a 'documents' key containing a list of items, each with 'document_title', 'document_type', 'author', 'date', and 'project_name'.

steps.name: Plan document processing approach
steps.description: Read the Step 1 document review output file and the tool registry. For each identified document, determine which tool to use to extract or process it based on document type, content resolution quality (text vs image-based), and available tools. Determine whether format conversion is required (e.g. convert a scanned PDF to PNG for OCR). Produce a structured processing plan for each document.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with: (1) a 'processing_plan' key — a list where each entry has: 'filename' (matching a document from the Step 1 DocumentReview output file), 'document_type' (string), 'content_type' (one of: text, image, unsupported, error), 'text_quality' (one of: high, low, none), 'recommended_reader' (string), 'processing_tool_id' (a valid tool ID from the registry, e.g. tool-extract-pdf-content or tool-read-file-content), 'conversion_required' (boolean), 'conversion_type' (string such as pdf-to-png when conversion_required is true, otherwise null), 'extraction_method' (non-empty string), and 'processing_notes' (string); (2) a 'total_documents' integer equal to the count of entries in processing_plan; (3) an 'output_file' string path to the saved ProcessingPlan_*.json file. For documents where content_type is 'image' or text_quality is 'none', conversion_required must be true. All processing_tool_id values must reference a tool defined in the registry.

steps.name: Extract information from design brief documents
steps.description: Using the processing plan output from Step 2, extract structured information from each identified design brief document. Apply the process step definitions from the 'Extract Information from Design Brief' process (proc-extract-design-brief-info) as the extraction guide. For each document, split the content into sections, score each section's relevance to the process step, tag sections with content categories (e.g. hv, lv, sl, earthing, easements, substation, funding), and produce a structured extraction grouped by category. The extraction must cover: (1) supply requirements — HV, LV, Street Lighting, Earthing, Easements, and Substation items; (2) determination of funding requirements — all items listed under this section including funding category, responsible party, and any stated amounts or formulas. Output one extraction JSON per document and a consolidated ExtractionReport.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with: (1) 'total_files' (integer) equal to the count of documents in the Step 2 processing plan; (2) 'extractions' (array) — one entry per file, each with: 'filename' matching a document from the processing plan, 'total_sections' (integer), 'relevant_sections' (integer, count of sections with relevance_score >= 0.4), 'extracted_sections' (array) where each section has 'section_name' (string), 'page_number' (integer), 'content_tags' (array of strings), 'relevance_score' (float 0.0-1.0), and 'relevance_reason' (string), and 'step_extraction' (object) with at least one of the following category keys containing a non-empty array: 'hv', 'lv', 'sl', 'earthing', 'easements', 'substation', 'funding_items' — each item must have 'description' (string) and 'source_section' (string); (3) 'output_file' (string) path to the saved ExtractionReport_*.json file.

templates.name: Review and Identify Design Brief Documents
templates.description: Upload the design brief package and identify all submitted documents. Extract key metadata, classify each document by type, and flag any missing or incomplete items against standard brief requirements.

steps.name: Extract and analyse design brief documents
steps.description: Upload the design brief package (PDF, DOCX, or ZIP of documents) to the Content Reviewer Agent. Extract key metadata from each document including document title, document type (e.g. Architectural Brief, Structural Brief, Services Brief, Town Planning Report), author, date, project name, and project number. Produce a structured list of all identified documents.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with a 'documents' key containing a list of items, each with 'document_title', 'document_type', 'author', 'date', and 'project_name'.

steps.name: Plan document processing approach
steps.description: Read the Step 1 document review output file and the tool registry. For each identified document, determine which tool to use to extract or process it based on document type, content resolution quality (text vs image-based), and available tools. Determine whether format conversion is required (e.g. convert a scanned PDF to PNG for OCR). Produce a structured processing plan for each document.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with: (1) a 'processing_plan' key — a list where each entry has: 'filename' (matching a document from the Step 1 DocumentReview output file), 'document_type' (string), 'content_type' (one of: text, image, unsupported, error), 'text_quality' (one of: high, low, none), 'recommended_reader' (string), 'processing_tool_id' (a valid tool ID from the registry, e.g. tool-extract-pdf-content or tool-read-file-content), 'conversion_required' (boolean), 'conversion_type' (string such as pdf-to-png when conversion_required is true, otherwise null), 'extraction_method' (non-empty string), and 'processing_notes' (string); (2) a 'total_documents' integer equal to the count of entries in processing_plan; (3) an 'output_file' string path to the saved ProcessingPlan_*.json file. For documents where content_type is 'image' or text_quality is 'none', conversion_required must be true. All processing_tool_id values must reference a tool defined in the registry.

steps.name: Extract information from design brief documents
steps.description: Using the processing plan output from Step 2, extract structured information from each identified design brief document. Apply the process step definitions from the 'Extract Information from Design Brief' process (proc-extract-design-brief-info) as the extraction guide. For each document, split the content into sections, score each section's relevance to the process step, tag sections with content categories (e.g. hv, lv, sl, earthing, easements, substation, funding), and produce a structured extraction grouped by category. The extraction must cover: (1) supply requirements — HV, LV, Street Lighting, Earthing, Easements, and Substation items; (2) determination of funding requirements — all items listed under this section including funding category, responsible party, and any stated amounts or formulas. Output one extraction JSON per document and a consolidated ExtractionReport.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with: (1) 'total_files' (integer) equal to the count of documents in the Step 2 processing plan; (2) 'extractions' (array) — one entry per file, each with: 'filename' matching a document from the processing plan, 'total_sections' (integer), 'relevant_sections' (integer, count of sections with relevance_score >= 0.4), 'extracted_sections' (array) where each section has 'section_name' (string), 'page_number' (integer), 'content_tags' (array of strings), 'relevance_score' (float 0.0-1.0), and 'relevance_reason' (string), and 'step_extraction' (object) with at least one of the following category keys containing a non-empty array: 'hv', 'lv', 'sl', 'earthing', 'easements', 'substation', 'funding_items' — each item must have 'description' (string) and 'source_section' (string); (3) 'output_file' (string) path to the saved ExtractionReport_*.json file.

templates.name: Extract Information from Site Plans
templates.description: Identify site plan documents in a project folder and extract structured substation data including asset number, transformer size, HV/LV switchgear, voltage level, cubicle size, and earthing information.

steps.name: Identify and Review Site Plan Documents
steps.description: Scan the project document folder for site plan drawings and electrical network diagrams. Identify all relevant files, classify each by type (site plan, substation drawing, network diagram), and produce a structured document list with metadata.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with a 'documents' key containing a list of matched site plan or drawing files, each with 'document_title', 'document_type', 'confidence', and 'filename'.

steps.name: Plan document processing approach
steps.description: Read the Step 1 document review output file and the tool registry. For each identified site plan document, determine the appropriate processing tool based on content type and text quality. Flag image-based drawings for OCR conversion.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with a 'processing_plan' key — a list where each entry has 'filename', 'processing_tool_id', 'conversion_required', 'content_type', 'text_quality', and 'extraction_method'.

steps.name: Extract information from design brief documents
steps.description: Using the processing plan from Step 2, extract structured site plan data from each identified document. Apply the process step definitions from the 'Extract Information from Site Plans' process (proc-extract-site-plan-info) as the extraction guide. For each document extract: substation asset number, transformer size, HV switchgear (applying the 1.5MVA â†’ Siemens RLR rule), voltage level, LV switchgear, cubicle size, and earthing information from substation notes and drawing annotations.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_files' and 'extractions' array. Each extraction must include 'filename' and 'sub_step_extractions' with keys for substation, transformer, hv-switchgear, voltage, lv-switchgear, cubicle, and earthing data. Output file path must be present.

templates.name: Review Customer Connections Application
templates.description: Full review of a Customer Connections Application — identifies all relevant documents, extracts design brief and site plan information in parallel, then consolidates results into a unified report.

steps.name: Identify and Review All Application Documents
steps.description: Scan the project document folder for all relevant documents. Use the search context and document categorisation rules from the process knowledge files (proc-extract-design-brief-info, proc-extract-site-plan-info, and proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet) to determine which files to include and how to classify each document type. For each file found, read the document content and classify it as one of: 'Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', 'Town Planning Report', 'Reticulation Drawing', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Diagram', 'Network Diagram', or 'TAL'. Any Excel spreadsheet file (.xlsx or .xls) containing asset register, equipment schedule, or inventory data (columns for Asset ID, Asset Type, Location, Condition Rating, or similar) must be classified as 'TAL' (Technical Asset List). Classification must be based on document content — what the document actually contains — not its filename. Produce a structured document list with metadata for all matched files.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with a 'documents' key containing a list of all matched files, each with 'document_title', 'document_type', 'confidence', and 'filename'. The 'document_type' field must reflect what the document actually contains based on a content review, not the filename. Spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls) containing asset register or equipment schedule data must have document_type set to TAL.

steps.name: Plan document processing approach
steps.description: CRITICAL OUTPUT REQUIREMENT: Every entry in the 'processing_plan' array MUST include a 'document_category' field set to exactly 'Design Brief', 'Site Plan', or 'TAL'. This field is mandatory — omitting it will cause step failure. Read the Step 1 document review output file and the tool registry. For each identified document produce a processing plan entry with ALL of the following fields: 'filename', 'document_type', 'content_type', 'text_quality', 'recommended_reader', 'processing_tool_id', 'conversion_required', 'conversion_type', 'extraction_method', 'processing_notes', 'document_category', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', and 'estimated_chunks'. For spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) with document_category 'TAL', the processing plan entry will also contain 'worksheet_count' (total sheets in the file), 'worksheets_to_analyse' (non-empty sheets), and 'worksheets' (array of {sheet_name, row_count, column_count}) — these are pre-populated by the agent and must be preserved exactly as provided in the output. To assign 'document_category', apply these rules IN ORDER: RULE 0 - TAL SPREADSHEET: if the file extension is .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb, set document_category to 'TAL' regardless of any other rules. RULE 1 — SITE PLAN FILENAME: if the filename contains any of 'RETIC', 'retic', 'DWG', 'dwg', 'SITEPLAN', 'DRAWING', 'SUBSTATION', set document_category to 'Site Plan'. RULE 2 — DESIGN BRIEF FILENAME: if the filename contains any of 'BRIEF', 'brief', 'ARP', 'DESIGN_BRIEF', 'ARCHITECTURAL', 'SERVICES', 'TOWN_PLANNING', set document_category to 'Design Brief'. RULE 3 — CONTENT FALLBACK (only when filename has no clear signal): use document_type from Step 1 — 'Reticulation Drawing', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Diagram', or 'Network Diagram' maps to 'Site Plan'; 'Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', or 'Town Planning Report' maps to 'Design Brief'; 'TAL' maps to 'TAL'. PAGE SIZE AND CHUNKING ASSESSMENT: Open each document and inspect the actual page dimensions. Classify page_size as one of: 'A4' (210Ã—297mm), 'A3' (297Ã—420mm), 'A2' (420Ã—594mm), 'A1' (594Ã—841mm), 'A0' (841Ã—1189mm), or 'large-format' (anything larger). Set requires_chunking to true if page_size is 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format' — these pages are too large for a single LLM context pass and must be split. For requires_chunking true, set chunk_strategy to 'quadrant-split' for A2/A1/A0/large-format drawings (split page into overlapping quadrants), or 'page-split' for multi-page text documents where individual pages exceed A3. Set estimated_chunks to the expected number of chunks per page (A2â†’4, A1â†’6, A0â†’9, large-formatâ†’12; use 1 for A4/A3). Set chunk_strategy to 'none' and estimated_chunks to 1 when requires_chunking is false. Example output entry: {"filename": "DS1_DAR1988_RETIC.pdf", "document_type": "Reticulation Drawing", "content_type": "image", "text_quality": "none", "recommended_reader": "ocr", "processing_tool_id": "tool-extract-pdf-content", "conversion_required": true, "conversion_type": "pdf-to-png", "extraction_method": "ocr", "processing_notes": "A1 large-format drawing, requires OCR with quadrant chunking", "document_category": "Site Plan", "page_size": "A1", "requires_chunking": true, "chunk_strategy": "quadrant-split", "estimated_chunks": 6} ROUTING RULES (set route_to_step in every processing plan entry): TAL files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) set route_to_step=6, requires_chunking=false, chunk_strategy="none" — they bypass Steps 3, 4, and 5 and go directly to Step 6 (asset spreadsheet extraction). Design Brief files set route_to_step=4. Site Plan files set route_to_step=5. Add "route_to_step" as a mandatory field in every processing plan entry.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with a 'processing_plan' key (array) and 'total_documents' integer. MANDATORY: every entry in 'processing_plan' must contain ALL of these fields: 'filename', 'processing_tool_id', 'conversion_required', 'content_type', 'text_quality', 'extraction_method', 'document_category', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', and 'estimated_chunks'. The 'document_category' field must be exactly 'Design Brief', 'Site Plan', or 'TAL'. Files with RETIC/DWG/SITEPLAN/DRAWING/SUBSTATION in their filename must have document_category 'Site Plan'. Files with BRIEF/ARP/DESIGN_BRIEF/ARCHITECTURAL/SERVICES/TOWN_PLANNING in their filename must have document_category 'Design Brief'. The 'page_size' field must be one of: 'A4', 'A3', 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. The 'requires_chunking' field must be true for any entry where page_size is 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. When requires_chunking is true, 'chunk_strategy' must be 'quadrant-split' or 'page-split' (not 'none') and 'estimated_chunks' must be greater than 1. Files with extension .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb must have document_category 'TAL'. TAL entries must also include 'worksheet_count' (integer), 'worksheets_to_analyse' (integer), and 'worksheets' (array). A missing or null 'document_category' is a critical failure. Every entry must also include 'route_to_step' (integer: 4 for Design Brief, 5 for Site Plan, 6 for TAL). TAL entries must have requires_chunking=false and chunk_strategy="none".

steps.name: Chunk Documents for Processing
steps.description: Read the Step 2 processing plan and identify all documents where requires_chunking is true AND route_to_step is NOT 6. TAL spreadsheet files (route_to_step=6) do not require chunking and must be excluded from this step — they are processed directly in Step 6. For each such document, call the document chunker with the document's chunk_strategy and page_size to render it into a sequence of PNG image chunks. A quadrant-split A1 drawing produces 6 PNG chunks (3 cols Ã— 2 rows); an A0 drawing produces 9 chunks (3Ã—3); a large-format drawing produces 12 chunks (4Ã—3). Each chunk is a distinct region of the page at 200 DPI. Output a chunk manifest per document listing all generated chunk files in sequence order with their region labels, dimensions, and file paths. Documents where requires_chunking is false are passed through unchanged and listed in documents_not_chunked.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_documents_chunked' (integer â‰¥ 0), 'documents_not_chunked' (list of filenames), and 'chunked_documents' (object keyed by filename). Each entry in 'chunked_documents' must include 'job_id', 'chunk_strategy', 'page_size', 'total_chunks', 'total_pages', and a 'chunks' array. Each chunk entry must have 'chunk_id', 'sequence', 'page_number', 'region', 'filename', 'filepath', 'width_px', and 'height_px'. Documents with requires_chunking=false must not appear in 'chunked_documents'. IMPORTANT: estimated_chunks_per_page in the input is the expected chunk count PER PAGE — the correct total_chunks equals estimated_chunks_per_page multiplied by total_pages; do not flag a mismatch if total_chunks equals estimated_chunks_per_page x total_pages. DPI is an internal rendering setting and must NOT be validated against any expected value.

steps.name: Extract Design Brief Information
steps.description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=4 (document_category="Design Brief"). TAL spreadsheet files and Site Plan files must be excluded. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those documents that meet the search context of the 'Extract Information from Design Brief' process (proc-extract-design-brief-info). A document qualifies if its content matches the search context criteria: it must contain a 'Method of Supply Requirement' section and/or a 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section, or be an architectural, services, or town planning report. Do not process any document that does not meet this content criteria, even if it was tagged as 'Design Brief' in Step 2 — and do not process any Site Plan or drawing document. For each qualifying document, extract structured information using proc-extract-design-brief-info as the extraction guide. Extract supply requirements (HV, LV, Street Lighting, Earthing, Easements, Substation) and funding determination items.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_files' and 'extractions' array containing only documents whose content matches the proc-extract-design-brief-info search context (i.e. documents containing a Method of Supply or Determination of Funding Requirements section). Must not include Site Plan, drawing, or reticulation documents. Each extraction must include 'filename', 'total_sections', 'relevant_sections', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', 'estimated_chunks', and 'step_extraction' with at least one of: hv, lv, sl, earthing, easements, substation, funding_items. The 'page_size' field must be one of: 'A4', 'A3', 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. When 'requires_chunking' is true, 'chunk_strategy' must be 'quadrant-split' or 'page-split' and 'estimated_chunks' must be greater than 1.

steps.name: Extract Site Plan Information
steps.description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan"). TAL spreadsheet files and Design Brief files must be excluded. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those where 'document_category' is 'Site Plan'. Do not process any document tagged as 'Design Brief'. For each Site Plan document, run the two-phase extraction defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info. Phase 1 extracts all numbered notes and callout annotations (ATTENTION/WARNING/CAUTION/NOTICE) from every page of the drawing in full. Phase 2 analyses those extracted notes to produce four structured checks: (1) Substation Data — extract the seven Substation Notes fields (Substation Asset Number, Transformer Size, HV Switchgear with 1.5MVA Siemens RLR override rule, Voltage Level, LV Switchgear, Cubicle Size, Earthing); (2) Assets Field Check Compliance — verify note 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' is present and answered YES; (3) Easement Restriction — check for the swimming pool restriction-on-use-of-land easement note; (4) Easement Substation — check for the padmount substation easement note with dimensions and lot number.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_files' and 'extractions' array containing only documents where 'document_category' is 'Site Plan'. Must not include any Design Brief documents. Each extraction must include 'filename', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', 'estimated_chunks', and 'sub_step_extractions'. The 'sub_step_extractions' object must contain all of the following keys: 'sub-step-extract-all-notes' (complete numbered notes from every page — value must be a non-empty string containing at minimum the first numbered note), 'sub-step-substation-data' (multi-line formatted string with exactly these seven field labels: Substation Asset Number, Transformer Size, HV Switchgear, Voltage Level, LV Switchgear, Cubicle Size, Earthing — each followed by a colon and a value or NOT FOUND), 'sub-step-field-check' (one of: YES, NOT COMPLETED, NO — NON-COMPLIANT, NOT FOUND — MISSING: this compliance note is mandatory), 'sub-step-easement-restriction' (verbatim note text with lot number, or NOT REQUIRED, or NOT FOUND — REQUIRED), 'sub-step-easement-substation' (verbatim note text with easement dimensions and lot number, or NOT REQUIRED, or NOT FOUND — REQUIRED). The 'page_size' field must be one of: 'A4', 'A3', 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. When 'requires_chunking' is true, 'chunk_strategy' must be 'quadrant-split' or 'page-split' and 'estimated_chunks' must be greater than 1. For 'sub-step-substation-data': NOT FOUND values for all fields are acceptable for pole transformer projects that have no padmount substation notes block — do not fail this check solely because all values are NOT FOUND. For 'sub-step-field-check': NOT COMPLETED is a valid result when the note is present but the YES/NO answer has not been filled in by the designer. VARIANCE TOLERANCE: the variance validator applies fuzzy similarity matching (threshold 97%) to long text fields such as 'sub-step-extract-all-notes' — minor punctuation differences, trailing conjunctions (e.g. ', or' vs ','), and equivalent whitespace variations are absorbed and do not count as unique values. Only genuine content differences (e.g. missing or reordered notes, truncated text with similarity below 97%) are flagged as inconsistencies. Structured short fields (substation data values, field-check result, easement text) use exact matching and must be identical across all runs.

steps.name: Extract Drawing Legend
steps.description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan") — the same document set as step 5. For each Site Plan document processed in step 5, extract the complete drawing legend (key) from every page using the vision extraction pipeline defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info step 4. Scan every image chunk of each drawing for a LEGEND, KEY, NOTATION, or SYMBOL TABLE panel. Process every visible row in the legend from top to bottom — do NOT stop at the end of one section before checking for additional sections; many legends have 2–4 sections separated by dividers (cables, equipment, boundaries, duct arrangements). For each visual element in the legend extract: a plain-English description of the symbol or line style, the exact text label as written on the drawing, a category (one of: cable/equipment/substation/boundary/earthing/annotation/other — REQUIRED on every entry without exception; if uncertain use 'other'), and the page number it was found on. STRICT SCHEMA: each legend entry object must contain EXACTLY these four fields — page, symbol_description, label, category. STRICT FIELD PROHIBITION: Any field name other than these four is prohibited — including but not limited to legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, id, section, type, geometry, or any other key. Category 'earthing' applies to earth conductors and earth electrodes — the label EARTHING maps to category 'earthing', not 'other'. DUCT ARRANGEMENT SECTION: Some drawings have a duct cross-section diagram inside the legend panel with rows like DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, DUCT WITH NEW CABLE, NEW TRENCH, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, SPARE DUCT, EARTHING, DUCT SPACING NTS — extract every one of these as a regular legend entry (category 'cable' for duct/trench/cable rows, 'earthing' for EARTHING, 'other' for DUCT SPACING NTS annotations). Assemble all entries across all chunks and all pages into a single structured list per document.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_files' and 'extractions' array containing only Site Plan documents. Each extraction must include 'sub_step_extractions' with the key 'sub-step-extract-legend'. The value of 'sub-step-extract-legend' must be a non-empty JSON array of legend entry objects. Each entry must have EXACTLY four fields: 'page' (integer, 1-based), 'symbol_description' (non-empty string describing the visual symbol or line style — not a label paraphrase), 'label' (non-empty string — the exact verbatim text label from the drawing), and 'category' (string — REQUIRED on every entry, must be one of: cable, equipment, substation, boundary, earthing, annotation, other; NEVER null or absent — use 'other' if uncertain). Category MUST be assigned using pattern rules: 'cable' if label contains TRENCH/CABLE/CONDUCTOR/OVERHEAD MAINS/UNDERGROUND MAINS/DUCT; 'equipment' if label contains POLE/PILLAR/COLUMN/LANTERN/SLCP/SHACKLE/JOINT/UGOH/HV ABS/HV USL/LV LINK or is a short equipment code; 'substation' if label contains SUBSTATION/KIOSK; 'boundary' if label contains EASEMENT/DEMARCATION/BOUNDARY/LGA; 'earthing' if label is EARTHING; otherwise 'other'. Entries with extra fields beyond these four (such as legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, or any other key) indicate schema drift and must be flagged. The array must contain at least one entry. Entries with null or empty symbol_description or label indicate incomplete extraction and must be flagged.

steps.name: Extract Asset Spreadsheet Data
steps.description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=6 (document_category="TAL"). TAL files do not require chunking — run this step directly after Step 2 without needing Steps 3, 4, or 5. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those entries with document_category of exactly "TAL" (Technical Asset List). These are the spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) identified in Step 1. For each TAL document, use the proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet process as the extraction guide. Each spreadsheet entry from Step 2 includes worksheet_count (total sheets), worksheets_to_analyse (non-empty sheets), and a worksheets array listing each sheet name with its row_count and column_count — use these to plan how many extraction passes are needed. For each non-empty worksheet: identify the header row, map column names to standard asset fields (Asset ID, Serial Number, Asset Type, Description, Location, Address, Feeder, Voltage Level, Capacity Rating, Manufacturer, Model, Condition Rating, Status, Installation Date, Last Inspection Date), and extract every data row as a structured asset record. Include source_sheet and source_document in every record. Produce a consolidated output with all asset_records across all sheets and all TAL files. OUTPUT FILE: The agent saves a dedicated AssetExtract_{timestamp}.json file to the job output directory. This file contains: process_id, timestamp, source_documents (list of spreadsheet filenames), total_assets (integer), and asset_records (flat array of all extracted asset records across all sheets and all TAL files). This file is stored in state as asset_extraction and is available for use by Step 7 (report consolidation) and any subsequent steps.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with "total_files" integer and "extractions" array. Each extraction entry must include "filename", "total_sections", "relevant_sections", and "step_extraction" containing "asset_records" (array), "total_assets" (integer), and "source_documents" (array). Every asset_record must have at minimum: asset_id or serial_number (at least one non-null), asset_type, and source_sheet. Only documents with document_category "TAL" must be processed — no PDF or Design Brief files. The response must also include asset_extract_file (path to AssetExtract_*.json) and asset_extract.total_assets (integer count of all extracted asset records).

steps.name: Match Assets to Site Plan Drawing Symbols
steps.description: Using the asset records from Step 7, the text annotations extracted from the site plan in Step 5 (sub-step-extract-all-notes), and the drawing legend from Step 6, locate each asset in the site plan drawing text and identify which legend symbol is associated with it. For each asset record: (1) Strip all leading alphabetic prefix characters and any leading zeros from the asset_id to produce a bare numeric ID (e.g. SLPL00302876 → 302876, CB00045 → 45, TX0001234 → 1234). (2) Search the Step 5 extracted site plan text for the bare numeric ID as a text token or label — asset IDs appear as numeric labels adjacent to their drawing symbols in the annotated text. (3) When found, examine the text context immediately surrounding the bare ID to infer which drawing symbol is associated with it, then match that context to the best-fitting legend entry from Step 6 (comparing symbol descriptions and labels). Set symbol_description and label from the matched legend entry and match_status to 'found'. (4) If the bare ID is not found in the extracted text, set symbol_description and label to null and match_status to 'not_found'. Output an updated_assets array containing every original asset record from Step 7 with four new fields added: bare_id, symbol_description, label, and match_status. Also output asset_symbol_matches (the raw per-asset match results) and match_summary totals.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'updated_assets' (array), 'asset_symbol_matches' (array), and 'match_summary' (object). 'updated_assets' must contain every original asset record from Step 7 with four additional fields: 'bare_id' (numeric-only string, no prefix letters, no leading zeros), 'symbol_description' (string or null), 'label' (string or null), and 'match_status' (exactly 'found' or 'not_found'). All original TAL fields must be preserved. 'asset_symbol_matches' must contain one entry per asset with 'asset_id', 'bare_id', 'symbol_description', 'label', and 'match_status'. 'match_summary' must include 'total', 'found', and 'not_found' integer counts that sum to total. A not_found count equal to total is acceptable when no asset IDs appear in the extracted site plan text — this is not a failure. Entries with match_status 'found' must have non-null symbol_description and label.

steps.name: Enrich Assets with Legend Data
steps.description: Using the asset records extracted in Step 7 and the symbol matches identified in Step 8, produce a fully enriched asset register. For each asset record, merge the original TAL fields with the matched symbol data from Step 8: add symbol_description (visual drawing symbol description), label (legend label text such as NEW LANTERN, EXISTING POLE, REMOVE LANTERN), and match_status (found/not_found). Where Step 8 returned match_status 'not_found', fall back to matching by comparing asset_type and description text against the drawing legend entries from Step 6, and set match_confidence to reflect whether the match came from the drawing (high) or from text inference (medium/low/none). Output a consolidated enriched asset list with all original TAL fields preserved plus the enrichment columns.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'enriched_assets' (array) and 'match_summary' (object). Every asset record from Step 7 must appear in 'enriched_assets'. Each entry must contain all original TAL fields plus: 'symbol_description' (string or null), 'label' (string or null), 'legend_label' (string or null), 'legend_category' (string or null), and 'match_confidence' (one of: high, medium, low, none). 'match_summary' must include 'total', 'found_on_drawing', 'inferred', and 'not_found' integer counts that sum to total. Assets whose symbol was identified from the drawing (via Step 8) must have match_confidence 'high'. A not_found count equal to total is acceptable when Step 8 found no drawing matches — do not fail solely on this basis.

steps.name: Consolidate Application Review Report
steps.description: Merge the design brief extraction (Step 4), site plan extraction (Step 5), and enriched asset register (Step 9) into a single unified Customer Connections Application Review report. Summarize all extracted supply requirements, funding items, site plan data, and the enriched asset list (with symbol labels identifying each asset as NEW, EXISTING, or REMOVE) in a consolidated JSON report.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'report_type', 'generated_at', 'source_extractions' (containing keys for design brief, site plan, asset extraction, and enriched asset results), and a 'summary' section with 'total_documents_processed' and 'extraction_keys'.

steps.name: Customer Connections Review Analysis
steps.description: Using all extracted inputs from the Design Brief (Step 4), Site Plan (Steps 5–6), and enriched asset register (Step 9), run a structured four-task Customer Connections Review. Task 1: Consolidate all determination of funding requirements from the Design Brief into a structured funding details section. Task 2: Compare the method of supply requirements from the Design Brief against the Site Plan and list any scope missing from the site plan. Task 3: Compare the funding arrangement outlined in the Design Brief against the Design Drawing and list any scope missing from the drawing. Task 4: Produce a complete asset register listing every asset with its legend information, action status (new/remove/replace/existing), and found-on-diagram status.

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'analysis_report' containing four keys: 'funding_details' (object with contestable/non-contestable/ancillary cost arrays), 'method_of_supply_comparison' (object with missing_from_site_plan array), 'funding_arrangement_comparison' (object with missing_from_drawing array), and 'asset_register' (array of asset records each with legend_label, action_status, match_confidence, found_on_diagram). 'consolidated_report_path' (string) must be present linking to the Step 10 completion test report. A partial result with some tasks completed is acceptable — do not fail solely on missing tasks if the analytics agent returned data.
